channel.title: +MT on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +MT on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Gupta highs
item.description: Against the grain of mainstream occidentally inspired historians of the current era who are big on downsizing gupta-s, I’d say the gupta run was unique for having a 4-5 generation excellence. In classical historical India it exceeded there was never one like it before or after. In many ways, it was a defining event for the H nation beginning or completing the transition from: the vaidika metaphor of the state to the tAntro-pauranic 1; From the arthashAstric to the dharmashAstric state;+++(4)+++ multifocal to temple-centric urbanism;+++(4)+++ Localization to massive centralization in civil engineering & religious expertise.

item.title: iShvAku-line
item.description: Source: TW manu vaivasvata ikshvAku {son of manu, great Aryan conqueror} vikukshi kakutstha anenA pR^ithu pR^ishadAshva I Ardra yuvanAshva I shrAvasta bR^ihadAshva I kuvalayAshva dR^iDh.Ashva haryAshva I nikumbha saMhatAshva kR^ishAshva {receives secret weapons from kaushikas; invades Iran} prasenajit I yuvanAshva II* mAndhAtA* {slain in battle with lavaNa} purukutsa* {conquest of the sapta sindhu} trasadasyu* {conquest of narmada valley- river named after trasadasyu’s wife} sambhuta anaraNya {slain in battle with rakshas; ikshvAku kingdom destroyed} pR^ishadAshva II haryAshva II sumanA tridhanvA trayyAruNa¥ trishanku {kaushikas change the year begining to adjust for precession} harishacandra rohitAshva harita cancu vijaya ruruka vR^ika bAhu sagara {ikshvAku armies conquer the land till the Arabian sea} asamanjasa {popular rebellion and dethroning of the king} anshumAna {restores ikshvAku regime} dilIpa I bhagIratha {damming of the Ganga} shruta nAbhAga ambarIsha* {destroys the political power of the atris under durvAsA} sindhudvIpa ayutAshva R^ituparNa sarvakAma sudAsa mitrasaha ashmaka sarvakarmA mUlaka dasharatha I ilivila kR^itasharmA vishvasaha I dilIpa II dIrghabAhu raghu {the ikshvAku expansion-I conquest of avanti,magadha and anga} aja {reconquers the narmada basin} dasharatha rAmacandra {avenges anaraNya’s death; the ikshvAku expansion-II} kusha atithi nishadha nala nabha puNdarIka kshemadhanvA devAnIka ahInagu rUpa ruru pAripAtra dala chala uktha vajranAbha shankhanAbha vyUthitAshva vishvasaha II hiraNyanAbha pushya dhruvasandhi sudarshana agnivarNa {the ikshvAku empire collapses; reduced to the ayodhyA patch} shIghra maru prasUshruta sugandhi amarshha mahasvAna vishrutavAna bR^ihadbala {slain in the great kurukshetra war} bR^ihatkshaNa gurukshepa vatsa vatsavyUha prativyoma divAkara sahadeva bR^ihadAshva II bhAnuratha pratItAshva supratIka marudeva sunakshatra kinnara antariksha suvarNa sumitra amitrajit bR^ihadrAja dharmI kR^itanjaya vrAta raNanjaya sanjaya shAkya shuddhodhana siddhartha rAhula prasenajit II kshudraka {ayodhya regime ends; ikshvAkus move westwards; Aristotle’s OXYDRAECEA kingdom) kshulika suratha sumitra

item.title: Religious state
item.description: Source: TW IMO, a proper historical account of the Hindu state has never been written. Most authors have followed the Occidental tendency to view it in a purely secular sense and explain the religious side of it merely as a framework for justifying V1 and/or V2 power. However, we hold that there are at least three deeply entwinned elements: the religious, the “para-religious” dharma, and the secular elements. As H saw the unity of knowledge, these elements were not truly separable in the H mind.

item.title: Tantra popularity
item.description: As you have elaborated all kings of those times adopted and followed a tantric system. Can you throw some more light on the motivations behind it and how exactly it helped strengthen the state? It is difficult to answer this question briefly. I don’t know for sure why there was a growing interest in royal tAntrika rites. Based on the options if offered my mundane level proposal is that it could be adopted by a much broader swath of the warrior “guild” at many levels (sAmanta etc) rather than just the monarch (an emphasis of the vedic system).

item.title: Tantrik state collapse
item.description: Source: TW Many often ask us the question as to why major “Tantric states” failed. For the idea of a Tantric state see: https://manasataramgini.wordpress.com/2009/04/02/geopolitics-of-the-tantra-age-an-attempt-at-a-blunt-assessment/ https://manasataramgini.wordpress.com/2014/02/14/the-erotic-the-warlike-and-the-imperial-in-the-tantra-age/ Several of the questioners believe that it might be due to 1. An intrinsic defect in the organization of the Tantric state. 2. Something negative stemming from the focal deity of the Tantric state. 3. Degeneracy from Tantric rituals, primarily those of a sexual nature or involving the use of alcohol.

item.title: V2 vedic study
item.description: I saw this twt earlier & was tempted to respond but desisted because 1 could ask: if a v2 or v3 claims to have studied the veda how do we know he really did so.. If we set that aside we can cite the following unambiguous cases: shrauta rituals- many v2s have done it close to modernity. e.g. the ashvamedha of jayasiMha-II, his successors continued shrauta rites down to the 1900s.
